what_it_does: Records real model-load events (load time and resident VRAM) in an append-only JSONL file keyed by service key, then reads those records back to provide the latest measured VRAM per service and a text rollup comparing measured memory against registry estimates.
observations: Append-only JSONL log stored next to the source file via os.path.realpath
Uses only the Python stdlib (json, os, datetime)
Telemetry records include ts, service, load_seconds, resident_mb, estimate_mb, and optional model
learned_vram returns the most recent non-zero resident_mb for a service
rollups computes per-service load count, average load time, last measured VRAM, last estimate, and flags differences >1500 MB
_read silently skips blank or malformed JSONL lines
LOG path is computed once at import time
record returns the dict it just wrote
standouts: Self-instrumentation feedback loop: measured loads are meant to improve budget-gate estimates over time
Explicit estimate-drift flag in rollups (⚠ estimate off)
conventions: Module-level docstring explains the architectural purpose
Private helper prefixed with underscore for internal read logic
datetime ISO format with second precision
Formatted text table output in rollups
concerns: No file locking or concurrency control for append-only writes
No log rotation or size limits; _read loads the entire file into memory
No input validation on numeric fields
LOG path resolved at import time can be surprising if the file is symlinked or moved
notes: A small stdlib-only telemetry logger that turns real model-load measurements into per-service VRAM and load-time rollups to refine registry estimates.
questions: Which component calls learned_vram and how does it override the registry estimate?
Where does estimate_mb come from when record is called?
What is the exact service-key namespace and who defines it?
Is there a retention or rotation policy for telemetry.jsonl?
How are concurrent writes from multiple processes handled?
Should the 1500 MB drift threshold be configurable?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it records real loads and surfaces registry estimate discrepancies.
intention_signals: Build a measured-memory feedback loop so budget-gate estimates improve over time and wrong registry estimates become visible.
novelty: The explicit learned_vram fallback that prefers measured over registry estimate, plus the built-in estimate-drift flag in rollups.
contribution: Provides a lightweight, dependency-free write-half of a telemetry feedback loop: append-only JSONL persistence, per-service learned VRAM lookup, and human-readable rollup reporting with an estimate-drift flag.
summary_for_embedding: A stdlib-only append-only JSONL telemetry module that records per-service model load times and resident VRAM, exposes the latest measured VRAM, and prints per-service rollups comparing measured memory against registry estimates.
intention_for_embedding: To give the resource manager measured load data so budget-gate estimates can be corrected over time.

symbols.does: Appends a JSONL record of a model load event.
symbols.description: Writes a timestamped line containing service, load_seconds, resident_mb, estimate_mb, and optional model to telemetry.jsonl, then returns the record.

symbols.does: Reads and parses all valid JSONL lines from the telemetry log.
symbols.description: Loads the local telemetry.jsonl file, skipping blank or malformed lines, and returns a list of record dicts.

symbols.does: Returns the most recent measured resident VRAM for a service.
symbols.description: Filters records by service and non-zero resident_mb, then returns the resident_mb from the latest matching record so callers can prefer measured over registry estimates.

symbols.does: Produces a per-service text summary of loads, average load time, and measured vs estimated VRAM.
symbols.description: Aggregates telemetry by service, computes load count, average load time, last measured and estimated VRAM, and flags services whose estimate differs from measurement by more than 1500 MB.
